title: Architecture: 85-99.6% of substitutions across HLA loci are non-coding (descriptive K-class claim)

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Architecture: 85-99.6% of substitutions across HLA loci are non-coding (descript'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Per-locus, per-region substitution counts from feature-aligned sequence comparison across 22,259 HLA alleles.

statistics.name: non_coding_fraction_of_substitutions_per_locus
statistics.note: Architecture / curated claim: descriptive census, no inference test. K-class evidence.

inference.justification: Single conjunct: even the lowest-NC-fraction locus (HLA-A at 85%) is well over half. This trivially licenses the descriptive claim that non-coding substitutions dominate the HLA substitution census, which is the load-bearing observation for the entire HLA non-coding biophysics paper. Architecture claims have minimal inference burden — the pre-registration is essentially that the descriptive number falls in a sane range; the value of the claim is in the latent space as a corner-anchor for the HLA topic region.
inference.failure_mode: If s_min_nc_fraction fell below 0.5, the framing 'HLA variation is mostly non-coding' would be false. Observed minimum 0.85 — comfortably over.

conclusion.assertion: Across all six classical HLA loci (A, B, C, DRB1, DQB1, DPB1), 85-99.6% of substitutions in the IPD-IMGT/HLA v3.56.0 allele census are non-coding (UTR + intron). The non-coding compartment dominates the substitution count even in the most coding-heavy locus (HLA-A at 85%).

scope.scale_note: Whole-allele descriptive census. Per-region (5UTR-only, intron-only, 3UTR-only) decompositions are sibling claims.

confidence.note: K-class architecture claim; confidence reflects census completeness, not statistical inference.

external_assumptions.statement: IPD-IMGT/HLA v3.56.0 is sufficiently complete at the genomic-sequence level for all six classical loci to support per-locus non-coding fraction estimates within ~1 percentage point.
